purpose: Confirmatory temporal evaluation on the frozen 120-site cohort, plus a separately sealed external new-site validation

development_evidence.reproducibility_scope: conditional on the frozen canonical panel bytes; the original WTEMP/FLOW/Daymet/gridMET HTTP responses, requests, and retrieval timestamps used to create that legacy panel were not retained and cannot be represented as source-level replay evidence

primary_site_cohort.rule: the complete frozen 120-site development registry; no outcome-based replacement

required_order: final protocol, protocol seal, claim ledger/renderer/validator, source tree, dependency locks, and model-training code are committed
all temporal and station-agnostic five-seed model bundles and the model-suite registry are frozen and replayed
the model-suite registry is committed before external candidate metadata, external registry, or 2021-2023 predictor inputs are acquired
external candidate metadata and retrospective predictor inputs are acquired and frozen without outcome access
the opening authorization binds the earlier model-freeze commit, later input-evidence commit, and their exact artifact hashes
prelabel_chronology_contract.evidence_scope: repository-internal chronology for an honest owner; not proof against Git-history rewriting by the repository owner
prelabel_chronology_contract.fallback_interpretation_if_order_not_ver: transductive retrospective exploration; confirmation claims prohibited

primary_historical_input_contract.information_cutoff: issue_date_end; no target-date or post-issue values
primary_historical_input_contract.provisional_vintage_limitation: The as-issued provisional vintage that would have existed on each historical issue date cannot be reconstructed from the retrospective gridded products. Frozen retrieval responses prove the one-shot retrospective dataset, not an operationally available vintage.
required_evidence_before_label_opening: resolved feature schema showing every predictor and lag
raw or normalized immutable snapshots for every non-outcome retrospective input actually consumed
explicit statement that provisional as-issued vintages are unavailable
required_at_single_label_opening: immutable raw response and provenance for post-2020 WTEMP/FLOW history and outcomes; WLEVEL may be retained in the same raw response but is not consumed

daily_outcome_quality_contract.series_rule: retain every daily-mean series column and merge only across dates: zero finite series values means missing, exactly one means retain that value with its qualifier and series identifier, and two or more finite series values on the same date means set that parameter/date missing with reason MULTIPLE_FINITE_SERIES_CONFLICT; never average or select among simultaneous series and never replace the site
daily_outcome_quality_contract.series_identity_evidence: record every exact value/qualifier column identifier; for each retained value record the selected series identifier; and for every MULTIPLE_FINITE_SERIES_CONFLICT record every constituent series identifier, parsed finite value, and preserved raw qualifier together with immutable per-site/parameter conflict counts
daily_outcome_quality_contract.primary_value_policy: use every finite parsed daily-mean WTEMP and FLOW value irrespective of approval qualifier; qualifiers never select or replace sites, models, dates, or primary forecast keys
daily_outcome_quality_contract.wlevel_policy: retain WLEVEL and its qualifier only as raw provenance; WLEVEL is not a model input or a Route-A estimand
daily_outcome_quality_contract.qualifier_preservation: trim surrounding whitespace, preserve the remaining raw qualifier string exactly, and retain missing qualifiers as missing rather than imputing approval
daily_outcome_quality_contract.unknown_qualifier_policy: retain and count every unrecognized qualifier string without interpreting or excluding it

mandatory_quality_audit.output: immutable counts including retained finite values, missing values, finite values with missing qualifiers, and MULTIPLE_FINITE_SERIES_CONFLICT dates

approved_only_target_sensitivity.key_rule: from already-frozen common predictions, retain a target only when WTEMP_qualifier split on commas and stripped has the exact token set {'A'}
approved_only_target_sensitivity.history_and_flow_rule: do not refit, impute, or filter issue-date WTEMP/FLOW histories; this sensitivity concerns target-label status only
approved_only_target_sensitivity.comparisons: exactly the same five candidate/reference/horizon rows listed in primary_inference_contract.confirmatory_family, with no additional model or horizon chosen after opening
approved_only_target_sensitivity.effect: for each comparison, compute station RMSE on exact-A keys, retain stations with at least 100 such targets for both models, and report the median station-level candidate-minus-reference RMSE difference
approved_only_target_sensitivity.interval: the same whole-HUC2 percentile bootstrap with 10000 draws and the comparison's frozen bootstrap_seed; this interval is descriptive and is not a second confidence procedure for the primary estimand
approved_only_target_sensitivity.reporting: emit counts, point effects, and intervals only; emit no sign-flip p-value, no Holm value, no pass/fail decision, and never remove, add, or reinterpret a primary test

daily_outcome_quality_contract.daily_time_support: NWIS dates are site-local finalized daily-value labels; Daymet and gridMET have provider-specific day definitions, so Route A is a date-indexed retrospective evaluation rather than an issue-time operational replay
daily_outcome_quality_contract.sensor_and_datum_limitation: the raw series identifier is retained, but sensor continuity is not established; gage-height datum is not harmonized because WLEVEL is not consumed

secondary_archived_nwp_contract.lead_semantics: For each valid hour, previous_dayN is the forecast value aligned N times 24 hours before that valid hour. A UTC daily mean is therefore a rolling fixed-lead composite available by the end of issue_date, not the output of one identified model initialization.
secondary_archived_nwp_contract.daily_aggregation: arithmetic mean only when all 24 valid-hour values are finite; otherwise retain an unavailable row and hour count
secondary_archived_nwp_contract.request_partition: one stable site_no and one UTC calendar month per immutable raw snapshot

pre_label_amendments.trigger: official source availability and lead-semantics audit
pre_label_amendments.change: limit only the optional secondary GFS 1/3/7-day sensitivity registry to targets on or after 2021-03-30; leave the primary confirmation start unchanged at 2021-01-01
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the documented GFS archive begins in March 2021 and a 7-day fixed-offset target cannot exist until seven days after the first archived run; primary models do not consume this artifact

pre_label_amendments.change: bind the primary model to the actual seven-variable feature order WTEMP/FLOW/TEMP/PRCP/RHMEAN/DH/WDSP, remove WLEVEL as a consumed predictor, and replace non-executable draft variant labels with the exact one-factor controls implemented by Stage 9
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the frozen implementation never exposes WLEVEL to the Route-A sequence or tabular models, and confirmatory labels must name executable interventions rather than conceptual placeholders

pre_label_amendments.trigger: machine-readable protocol and trusted-scorer consistency audit
pre_label_amendments.change: make the already-declared threshold of 100 valid confirmation targets per frozen station and horizon explicit in the machine-readable availability contract
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the prose protocol already fixed this threshold, but relying on an implementation default would leave the scoring contract under-specified

pre_label_amendments.trigger: official Daymet and gridMET variable-metadata audit
pre_label_amendments.change: retain the frozen seven-column feature schema while defining DH as Daymet daylight-period mean shortwave flux, RHMEAN as a vapour-pressure/Tetens proxy, and WDSP as packed gridMET wind transformed only after freezing and validating the OPeNDAP units, scale_factor, and add_offset attributes
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the legacy feature names are required for executable model compatibility but must not be misrepresented as a 24-hour radiation mean, daily energy, measured daily mean relative humidity, or an unverified wind unit

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening environmental-science audit of NWIS qualifier, series, unit, sensor, datum, and calendar-day semantics
pre_label_amendments.change: freeze an inclusive finite-value primary policy, mandatory raw qualifier/series audit, and an exact-A target-only descriptive sensitivity that cannot alter the five confirmatory tests
pre_label_amendments.rationale: choosing an approved/provisional filter after seeing outcomes would create outcome-dependent attrition; preserving all values in the primary analysis while predeclaring a nonconfirmatory exact-A sensitivity exposes data-quality dependence without changing the estimand post hoc

pre_label_amendments.change: state the exact issue/target admissibility, frozen-imputation, common-key, 100-target reportability, station-balanced point estimand, and conditional-observability interpretation already implemented by the scorer
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the primary statistic is meaningful only if readers can distinguish performance on observable issue/target keys from performance on every site-day; spelling out the existing rule prevents later MNAR overgeneralization without changing any test

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening audit of the metadata-known 15-cluster canonical registry
pre_label_amendments.change: replace Monte-Carlo sampling of 50000 sign vectors with exhaustive enumeration of all 2^K configurations and predeclare equal-HUC and leave-one-HUC influence summaries as descriptive sensitivities
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the frozen registry has at most 15 HUC2 clusters, so exact enumeration is both cheaper than 50000 draws and removes avoidable Monte-Carlo error without changing hypotheses, margins, paired effects, or the Holm family

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening executable audit of the probabilistic and event-evaluation promises
pre_label_amendments.change: freeze the seasonal event reference, station-balanced descriptive metric aggregation, missing-head status, and exact probability artifact schema before confirmation outcomes are acquired
pre_label_amendments.rationale: Brier skill and reliability diagnostics cannot use the confirmation event rate as a reference, and leaving their aggregation or missing-model policy implicit would permit outcome-dependent reporting choices

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening audit of model-state replay and dependency resolution
pre_label_amendments.change: require an isolated exact replay of every frozen learned model on the frozen validation, calibration, and 2019--2020 development partitions, and bind the opening authorization to the deterministic replay receipt and a fully transitive Python 3.12 dependency lock with package hashes
pre_label_amendments.rationale: file existence and direct dependency pins cannot prove that all serialized model members reload to the pre-opening predictions or that a clean environment resolves the same dependency bytes; both checks must be completed before confirmation labels are acquired

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening audit of the legacy 2006--2020 development panel's source evidence
pre_label_amendments.change: state that development-model reproducibility is conditional on the frozen canonical panel bytes and cannot be called a source-level replay of the original provider responses
pre_label_amendments.rationale: the original WTEMP/FLOW/Daymet/gridMET HTTP response bytes, request records, and retrieval timestamps were not retained when the legacy panel was created; immutable panel bytes support downstream replay but do not retroactively supply missing raw provenance

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening audit of transductive adaptation and local evidence chronology
pre_label_amendments.change: require the full model suite to be frozen, replayed, and committed before external-site metadata or 2021--2023 predictor covariates are acquired, and bind authorization to replayable Git ancestry and blob hashes
pre_label_amendments.rationale: simultaneously binding models and target-cohort covariates does not establish that model choices preceded those covariates; a staged internal commit chain closes ordinary accidental transductive adaptation while explicitly not claiming an external timestamp or malicious-owner guarantee

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening adversarial audit of result-language generation
pre_label_amendments.change: freeze structured claim templates, exact five-test coverage, a joint Holm-and-confidence-interval support rule, evidence-conflict wording, and permanent exploratory/limitation scopes before outcomes
pre_label_amendments.rationale: regex matching and nonempty files cannot prove that a manuscript decision matches the receipt-bound statistics; deterministic rendering must treat disagreement between the predeclared inferential summaries conservatively and cannot turn non-inferiority into equivalence

pre_label_amendments.trigger: pre-opening failure-mode audit of the fixed multi-request NWIS transport
pre_label_amendments.change: permit create-only transport recovery under the same irreversible opening ID and exact request ledger, only before any normalization or trusted output exists, while prohibiting replacement of partial/inconsistent transactions, sites, requests, models, or statistics
pre_label_amendments.rationale: a transient failure among many fixed HTTPS requests should not force a statistically new opening; validating completed request transactions and fetching only absent ones preserves one analysis while still failing closed on any ambiguous partial write

metadata_candidate_contract.state_universe_rule: the 34 states represented by the frozen development cohort
metadata_candidate_contract.site_query: USGS stream sites, siteStatus=all, whose metadata advertises daily-value parameter 00010 capability

primary_estimand.population: the fixed 120-site canonical development registry, with no post-opening station replacement
primary_estimand.admissible_issue: issue_date and the selected horizon target_date are within 2021-01-01 through 2023-12-31, issue-date WTEMP is finite and observed, and a 32-day history window can be built; other missing inputs are filled only by frozen development-period transforms and retain missingness masks
primary_estimand.admissible_target: target-date WTEMP is finite and observed under the inclusive qualifier policy; target validity is evaluated independently for h=1,3,7
primary_estimand.model_comparison_keys: the exact intersection is fixed by the reconstructed admissible forecast registry and every declared model must produce one row on every such key
primary_estimand.station_horizon_reportability: at least 100 admissible target keys for that station and horizon
primary_estimand.within_station_metric: unweighted RMSE across that station/horizon's admissible common daily keys
primary_estimand.between_station_effect: median across reportable stations of RMSE(ThermoRoute)-RMSE(reference), so every retained station contributes one paired effect regardless of daily record count
primary_estimand.interpretation_limit: performance conditional on observable issue-date and target-date WTEMP under this availability-enriched cohort; not performance over every calendar day or every U.S. river

probabilistic_event_contract.eligible_models: every frozen bundle-backed model with finite q05, q50, q95, and p_exceed on the exact common forecast keys; deterministic built-ins are emitted as NOT_AVAILABLE_NO_PROBABILISTIC_HEAD rather than silently omitted
probabilistic_event_contract.ensemble_quantiles: member-wise averages followed by the frozen 2018 CQR offsets; these are engineering ensemble quantile summaries, not quantiles of a mixture distribution
probabilistic_event_contract.temporal_event: target WTEMP exceeds that station's q90 computed only on the 2006-2015 development training partition
probabilistic_event_contract.external_event: target WTEMP exceeds one pooled q90 computed only from all 2006-2015 development-training observations; this absolute statistical threshold is not site-local, biologically meaningful, ecologically comparable, or regulatory
probabilistic_event_contract.event_calibration: one frozen Platt calibrator per horizon fitted only on 2018 calibration forecasts; no confirmation label or confirmation event rate is used
probabilistic_event_contract.interval_calibration: frozen 2018 CQR offset by station and horizon for the temporal cohort and pooled by horizon for the external cohort

event_reference_climatology.temporal: station-by-calendar-month event probability under station train-q90, smoothed with weight 2 toward the station rate and then the global rate
event_reference_climatology.external: pooled calendar-month event probability under pooled train-q90, smoothed with weight 2 toward the pooled global rate; no external-site outcome adaptation
event_reference_climatology.brier_skill: 1 minus station-balanced Brier score divided by the station-balanced Brier score of this frozen seasonal reference; never use the confirmation event rate

probabilistic_event_contract.aggregation: within each cohort/model/horizon, retain stations with at least 100 exact common targets and weight every retained station equally by assigning each of its rows weight 1/n_station_rows
probabilistic_event_contract.undefined_metric_rule: AUROC, AUPRC, or calibration regression is null with an explicit reason when the retained evaluation outcomes have one class or the weighted fit is not estimable; do not substitute a value

primary_inference_contract.station_metric: RMSE on common daily forecast keys
primary_inference_contract.paired_effect_convention: station_RMSE_ThermoRoute minus station_RMSE_reference; negative is better
primary_inference_contract.primary_effect: median of paired station-level RMSE differences

confidence_interval.within_cluster_rule: retain all station effects together whenever a sampled HUC2 cluster is drawn
confidence_interval.unverified_huc_rule: each station lacking verified HUC metadata is a stable singleton cluster
confidence_interval.seed_rule: 1000 + horizon + (100 when reference is LightGBM, else 0)

one_sided_p_value.null_assumption: cluster-level sign symmetry of the paired station effects around the tested margin
one_sided_p_value.alternative: median paired effect is lower than the tested margin
one_sided_p_value.within_cluster_rule: apply one common random sign to every station effect in the HUC2 cluster
one_sided_p_value.unverified_huc_rule: each station lacking verified HUC metadata is a stable singleton cluster
one_sided_p_value.enumeration_rule: enumerate every one of the 2^K sign vectors for the K reportable HUC2/singleton clusters; the frozen cohort has at most 15 verified HUC2 clusters
one_sided_p_value.legacy_seed_field: retained in each structured test only as an inert backward-compatible identifier; it does not affect the exact p-value

exploratory_spatial_inference_sensitivit.comparisons: exactly the same five candidate/reference/horizon rows and reportable-station effects used by the primary family
exploratory_spatial_inference_sensitivit.equal_huc_effect: take the median station effect within each HUC2, then the median of those HUC2 medians so every HUC2 contributes one regional value
exploratory_spatial_inference_sensitivit.leave_one_huc: for each HUC2 in turn, remove all of its stations and recompute the station-weighted median paired effect; report effect-minus-margin but no inferential decision
exploratory_spatial_inference_sensitivit.limitations: HUC2 is a broad hydrologic region, not proof of independent river-network components; exact sign flipping still relies on cluster-level sign symmetry

confirmatory_family.description: ThermoRoute vs damped persistence superiority at horizon 1, margin 0.00 C
ThermoRoute vs damped persistence superiority at horizon 3, margin 0.00 C
ThermoRoute vs damped persistence superiority at horizon 7, margin 0.00 C
ThermoRoute vs LightGBM non-inferiority at horizon 3, margin +0.05 C
ThermoRoute vs LightGBM non-inferiority at horizon 7, margin +0.05 C

primary_inference_contract.noninferiority_margin_rationale: +0.05 degrees C was fixed before confirmation outcomes as a strict numerical ceiling on allowable degradation in the station-median RMSE difference. It is not derived from sensor precision, biological response, water-quality regulation, or a stakeholder utility function, and therefore carries no ecological or regulatory importance claim.

confirmatory_claim_decision_contract.supported_if_and_only_if: status is ESTIMABLE, Holm-adjusted one-sided p-value is at most 0.05, and the complete percentile confidence interval lies below the predeclared margin (ci_high_c < margin_c)
confirmatory_claim_decision_contract.evidence_conflict_rule: if the Holm decision and percentile confidence interval disagree, report EVIDENCE_CONFLICT_NOT_SUPPORTED together with both values; do not select the favorable procedure
confirmatory_claim_decision_contract.not_estimable_rule: report NOT_ESTIMABLE with frozen attrition counts and no directional conclusion
confirmatory_claim_decision_contract.noninferiority_wording_limit: H2 may say only that the result satisfies the predeclared +0.05 C numerical non-inferiority bound; it may not say equivalent, parity, equally good, ecologically negligible, or regulatorily acceptable
confirmatory_claim_decision_contract.negative_result_rule: NOT_SUPPORTED is not evidence of no difference, equivalence, or model failure outside the frozen estimand

primary_inference_contract.multiplicity: Holm step-down adjustment across exactly the five confirmatory one-sided tests

required_before_opening_labels: candidate discovery raw snapshot and checksum
isolated deterministic development replay receipt covering every frozen learned model member and head on validation, calibration, and 2019--2020 development keys
fully transitive Python 3.12 dependency lock with exact versions and package hashes
frozen structured claim ledger, deterministic renderer, automatic receipt-derived phase validator, and exact five-test decision templates
repository-internal chronology evidence proving the model-suite commit precedes external candidate metadata and 2021-2023 predictor acquisition
frozen primary metrics and hypothesis tests
frozen provenance and snapshots for every retrospective input actually consumed by the primary models
availability_contract.secondary_nwp_resolution_before_opening: either ACQUIRED_AND_FROZEN or EXPLICITLY_NOT_USED; acquisition is not a primary-label-opening gate

opening_transport_recovery_contract.resume_role: transport recovery within the same irreversible opening, not a second statistical opening
opening_transport_recovery_contract.existing_transaction_rule: validate exact request, response, metadata, and hashes for every completed create-only transaction; request only ledger entries with no transaction directory
opening_transport_recovery_contract.partial_transaction_rule: a partial or inconsistent request transaction is never overwritten and makes the opening indeterminate
opening_transport_recovery_contract.pre_resume_gate: acquisition manifest, normalized outcome panels, trusted predictions, statistics, report, and receipt must all be absent; otherwise automatic resume is prohibited
opening_transport_recovery_contract.pre_completion_visibility_boundary: fixed code does not parse, normalize, score, summarize, or render outcomes until every request transaction in the frozen ledger is complete; the honest filesystem owner can still read raw bytes and this is not a malicious-owner security boundary

external_new_gage_inference_contract.preprocessing: pooled transformations fitted only on the frozen 120-site development training partition
external_new_gage_inference_contract.model_suite: station-agnostic ThermoRoute, LSTM, and LightGBM plus persistence, pooled damped persistence, and pooled seasonal climatology
external_new_gage_inference_contract.availability_rule: report all frozen sites and attrition; do not replace a site using post-2020 availability or outcomes

honesty_guard: The protocol is frozen, but no post-2020 outcome data or new-site external cohort is bundled here. Do not report confirmatory results until verified acquisition artifacts and a one-time opening record exist.
